q: What is the key genetic difference between sexual and asexual reproduction?
options: Asexual reproduction produces offspring with double the genetic material of the parent
Sexual reproduction requires two parents and produces genetically unique offspring via meiosis and fertilization; asexual reproduction produces genetically identical clones via mitosis
Sexual reproduction always produces more offspring per cycle than asexual reproduction
Asexual reproduction involves meiosis; sexual reproduction involves mitosis of gametes
explanation: Sexual reproduction combines gametes from two parents, generating genetic variation through meiosis (crossing over, independent assortment) and random fertilization; asexual reproduction copies parental DNA exactly.
